title: Hindered Hearing
subtitle: 5 Things That Could Hold You Back From Hearing God
fullTitle: Hindered Hearing: 5 Things That Could Hold You Back From Hearing God
description: All humans have the need to be heard and understood. This attribute of God was placed in us. If God is the foundation of community then our ability to communicate with God will set the stage for all communication with other relationships. Your ability to converse with Father God, Jesus Christ and Holy Spirit will be in direct correlation to your ability to communicate on all other levels.

sessions.title: Clearing Your Reception
sessions.verse: So then faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
warmUp: Do you believe God speaks to people? If so, how does God speak to people?
Does God speak to you? If so, how?

content.text: All humans have the need to be heard and understood. This attribute of God was placed in us. It's not a prideful desire but a natural law of existence. To exist and have your existence noticed and enjoyed while enjoying the existence of others.

This is the foundation of all relationships. All communication stems from this need. Like being in a conversation with someone who you really enjoy, each of you hang on to the expressions made by the other. Just as a husband and wife who are completely in love.

This same desire to be heard and noticed can also be twisted against us. Maybe we were raised in environments of neglect and all of a sudden we begin changing who we are to become acceptable to those around us. We start to question the way God created us. Am I good enough to my earthly parents, society and ultimately God Himself?

Many of us are searching for a platform where our voices can be heard. This desire to spill our hearts on accepting ears is good yet far too often our voices only reach other souls attempting the same plight. And we never actually hear or get heard.
If God is the foundation of community then our ability to communicate with God will set the stage for all communication with other relationships.

If our relationship with God is not in proper alignment then our communication with others will be hindered. Your ability to converse with Father God, Jesus Christ and Holy Spirit will be in direct correlation to your ability to communicate on all other levels.

The bible says that we walk by faith and not by sight. This means that we make decisions based on the inward conversation with Holy Spirit. The bible also says that faith comes by hearing the living word of God. So in order for us to walk by faith we need to hear his direction. Spiritually we need ears to see where we are going.

questions: Why is it important that you grow in your ability to hear God speak?

content.text: I am the good shepherd, and I know My own, and My own know Me, just as the Father knows Me and I know the Father; and I lay down My life for the sheep. And I have other sheep that are not of this fold; I must bring them also, and they will listen to My voice; and they will become one flock, with one shepherd.

After conversing with someone for 5 minutes, you might recognize their voice over the phone a week later. But a month later you probably would not know the voice on the other side. Now if you talked to that person frequently you would immediately know their voice even if they were trying to impersonate someone else. And the more you get to know someone you begin picking up on their speech patterns and phrases. You know what kind of things they say and how they express themselves.

In John 10 Jesus, speaking of himself as the Good Shepherd, says the sheep have heard the shepherd's voice day after day until it is ingrained in who they are. If anyone comes dressed like a shepherd but doesn't sound like him, they will not follow. Simply through repetition, the practice of hearing His voice will make it easier to recognize His voice in the midst of many others.
God's voice will always line up with scripture from a new covenant viewpoint.

Another way that we can be deceived from recognizing his voice is not knowing the things He says. What I mean by this is, another spirit could tell you to come against someone who has wronged you. And you could back that up with old covenant scripture. However we are no longer under the old covenant but a new one where we are to "love our enemies."

Reading His word daily and learning the way Jesus communicated with people is key to our ability to recognize His voice inside of us. Maybe God is telling you to give one of your possessions away to someone in need. This lines up with scripture and the communication of Jesus. However, if another voice tells you to hold on to that item because you don't know how you will get another. Well that is called hoarding and is a root of the poverty mindset.

I consume His word everyday, even if it is only a few verses. I then practice asking questions in order to listen for wisdom and instruction. Again it is through repetition and daily consumption that I am able to recognize His voice more clearly as I walk in faith. Practice this by asking God questions and writing down the first things that come up. Does this sound like God or someone else?

questions: Hearing God speak with you in conversation form. Does this sound crazy, delusional or unreasonable? Or does this make you excited to spend more time with Him? Explain.
Sometimes God's voice can come through other people. Have you ever experienced this before? Share.

content.text: Wisdom strengthens a wise person more than ten rulers who are in a city. Indeed, there is not a righteous person on earth who always does good and does not ever sin. Also, do not take seriously all the words which are spoken, so that you do not hear your servant cursing you, or you know that even you have cursed others many times as well.

When you are offended, your mind and body begin to have certain reactions. You may get flustered, turn red and your nose may itch as your mind begins to think of ways to respond. You might even have daydreams of an altercation with this offender, thinking what you would say and how the encounter would play out.

If left unattended, offense can produce a root of bitterness with the option for full blown unforgiveness. Along this journey you will begin to entertain other voices who intend to encourage your bitterness to grow. Blinded by offense, your ability to hear God will be stifled with words of distraction. "How could they..." and "They should pay for blankety blank."
Offense not only has the ability to hinder our natural but also our spiritual appetites.

Have you ever been so offended by something or someone that you lose your appetite or worse even up chucked your last meal. Think about that last statement in the spiritual sense. In Matthew 4 Jesus said "man shall not live by bread alone but every word that proceeds from the mouth of God." God's living word, like manna from heaven, is our daily sustenance.

We are destroying sophisticated arguments and every exalted and proud thing that sets itself up against the [true] knowledge of God, and we are taking every thought and purpose captive to the obedience of Christ.

Our feelings may be justified according to the circumstance but that doesn't mean we allow our feelings to rule our appetites. It's ok to be hurt. It's ok to say that you are hurt. It is not ok to let that hurt reign over you.

Pay attention and always be on guard [looking out for one another]! If your brother sins and disregards God's precepts, solemnly warn him; and if he repents and changes, forgive him. Even if he sins against you seven times a day, and returns to you seven times and says, 'I repent,' you must forgive him [that is, give up resentment and consider the offense recalled and annulled].

questions: God, is there anyone I am holding offense to? If there is someone, ask Him to help you address and let it go. Let Him lead you through forgiveness.

content.text: Put on the full armor of God, so that you will be able to stand firm against the schemes of the devil.

In Genesis 3:8 the man and his wife have just eaten from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil and their eyes have been opened to their nakedness. The anointed covering of God had been removed so they hid themselves from Him. When questioned the man says "I heard the sound of You in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked; so I hid myself." And He said, "Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree of which I commanded you not to eat?"
It was never the disobedience itself that caused man to hide. It was the shame and condemnation brought on by the enemy that created the need to cover himself.

Likewise, when you fall short of perfect obedience to God the enemy is close by reminding you with accusations. All of a sudden you may begin to agree with these words and receive them as truth. As we can read, God questions this line of thinking in the man. Who told you that you were naked? Did I tell you that? And if It didn't come from Me why are you believing it?

When the voice of the enemy is more prevalent than God's, we will hide from our Father in fear of punishment. As the bible says, But perfect love casts out fear, because fear involves punishment, and the one who fears is not perfected in love. Adam was not perfected in love. "We" are being perfected in love. Which means we are still targets for the enemy to point out our short falls and remind us of our lack (or nakedness). This is why It is so important that we take every thought captive to the obedience of Christ.
Shame is the counterfeit of repentance.

Shame will cause us to hide from God in the shadows of failure. Repentance will thrust us into His arms with honest cries of "I need help." Shame will cause us to hear the move of God and shut our senses to His presence. Repentance will cause us to run to revival with an open heart that bears the wounds of insecurity.

Let us thank Jesus for removing every mistake, past present and future, so that we might stand before Him as if we never sinned. Tell the enemy and his accusations to hit the road. Shame no longer has a place here. Guilt no longer applies to you as you are now covered in the blood of Jesus, clothed in His righteousness and never to be naked again. He clothes you in the beauty of His word. Don't let anyone tell you how you should feel except for Him who made you.

questions: Ask Jesus, "Is there any shame in my life that is keeping me from running to you?" He will never put you down for hiding. He will always call you out of the hiding place and cover you with words of love.

content.text: And He said to them, "Come away by yourselves to a secluded place and rest a little while." (For there were many people coming and going, and they did not even have time to eat.) And they went away in the boat to a secluded place by themselves.

My wife and I have four children and often our days can be filled with details that make it hard for us to have meaningful conversations. We have to find time to invest in each other and discuss schedules in order to manage our family well. Even more intentional conversation is needed to share our dreams and desires for our future life. If we are not careful, weeks or worse, months can go by without an intimate heart connecting conversation.
Rest is the key. When we put everything away and come eye to eye, we allow ourselves to rest in each other.

All of the busyness can wait cause right now all my attention and affection is pointed in one direction. We don't need to be anywhere, we are detached from our devices and our hearts are open for business to trade life. Marriage is a great example of what our relationship with God should look and sound like. However a healthy sustained relationship with God is the result of an intentional daily decision to rest in Him.
It is in vain that you rise up early and go late to rest, eating the bread of anxious toil; for he gives to his beloved sleep.

Let's just be honest. You might be too busy to hear God clearly. You may know His voice and have lots of listening experience with God, but somehow face to face conversations have been replaced with busy passings by. Now you only catch half of the message from God and miss key details that could be the difference between ok and excellent. Has the daily routine of life robbed you of the ability to sit, rest in Christ and know that He is God? It's time to say goodbye to busyness and anxiety and hello to rest and supernatural peace. The shalom of the Lord is the Spirit that destroys chaos.

Do not be anxious about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.

questions: What would happen if you scheduled breakfast with Jesus, coffee with Holy Spirit or a walk with Father God? What questions would you ask?
Do you rest well? What does this look like for you? How do you include God in your rest time?

lessons.title: #5: Did You Hear Him the First Time?

content.text: As a dad I am called by God to lead and guide my kids into their destiny just as He is leading me. If my son asks me to teach him how to make the best shepherd's pie in heaven and earth, I will give him the exact recipe and the proper groceries to prepare the dish. However if he loses the recipe and lets the groceries go bad, I might be a little confused about his ability to follow through.
In the epistle of James he writes:

But be doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves. For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man who looks intently at his natural face in a mirror. For he looks at himself and goes away and at once forgets what he was like.
That word "doer" in this scripture is the Greek word poeta. It means maker, producer or performer.
This scripture makes it clear that the purpose of listening is to respond to what you hear and steward the information wisely.

Like my son who never followed through on the delicious recipe, you can ask God for guidance, receive the revelation and then never put it into action. Then go right back to Papa God asking for another recipe when you never produced the first. God may be saying to you, "How can I give you another assignment while the last one was stewarded poorly. How about you produce this first one and I will give you another." God is not guiding you with heavenly wisdom because it sounds good. No, He showers us with wisdom so that we can apply it and bear fruit as we abide in His love.
But one who looks intently at the perfect law, the law of liberty, and abides by it, not having become a forgetful hearer but an effectual doer, this man will be blessed in what he does.

What was the last assignment God gave you? Has He given you proper instruction to start? I assure you that if you steward His instruction well you find an endless supply of adventure and life assignments direct from the throne room. It is His desire to give you more but how can He give you the next recipe if you haven't completed the last. This is good parenting and moves us into a culture of diligence.
Faith without works is… Dead. Follow through on the last thing He said.

But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all generously and without reproach, and it will be given to him. But he must ask in faith without any doubting, for the one who doubts is like the surf of the sea, driven and tossed by the wind.

questions: Is God asking you to complete something He already gave you instruction on?
Do you feel like it's too late? Ask Him for wisdom in this area. Ask "Papa, what's next?" Then listen for his gentle response.
